---
title: "CISP 360 \n Structured Programming"
subtitle: "Introduction to C++"
author: "Dr. Caleb Fowler"
version: "1.0.1"
# Changelog:
# 1.0.0 (2026-03-13) - Week 9, Lecture 1: Compiler internals, types, overflow, casting

format:
  revealjs:
    margin: 0.05
    css: ../styles/styles.css
    theme: serif
    slide-number: true
    navigation-mode: vertical
    transition: slide
    incremental: false
    scrollable: false
    controls: true
    hash: true
    history: true
    title-slide: false
    footer: "CISP 360 · Fowler"
    keyboard: true
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script>
          window.Reveal.on('ready', event => {
            Reveal.configure({
              keyboard: {
                34: 'down',
                33: 'up',
                116: 'right',
                190: 'left',
                79: null
              }
            });
          });
          </script>

filters:
  - plantuml-filter.lua

engine: jupyter
execute:
  eval: true
  echo: true

resources:
 - topics/**/images/**

---

## Last Time

::: {.incremental}
1. **Course Philosophy**: Focuses on **Structured Programming** as a way of reasoning, emphasizing logical thinking, abstraction, and decomposition rather than just learning syntax.
2. **C++ Characteristics**: A modern, high-performance language that supports multiple "layers" of abstraction, allowing it to work close to the machine or look similar to high-level languages like Java.
:::


## Today's Agenda

- Overview of variable types
- Variables and assignment
- Arithmetic operators

::: notes

:::



{{< include topics/type_overview/type_overview.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/variables_n_assignment/variables_n_assignment.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/arithmetic_operators/arithmetic_operators.qmd >}}


# Key Takaways




# Next Time
